title: 10 best herbal capsules for anxiety
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for anxiety are dietary supplements that contain natural herbs and botanicals to help alleviate symptoms of anxiety. Examples of herbs commonly used in these capsules include chamomile, valerian root, passionflower, and lavender. These herbs have calming and relaxing properties that can help reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm. By taking herbal capsules for anxiety, individuals may experience an improvement in their overall quality of life, such as reduced stress and tension, improved sleep, and increased ability to cope with daily stressors. For instance, a person who previously struggled with anxiety-induced insomnia may find that taking herbal capsules containing valerian root helps them fall asleep faster and stay asleep longer, leading to a more restful night's sleep and improved mood the following day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsule helps with anxiety because it contains a natural compound called passiflora incarnata, which has been shown to have anxiety-reducing effects. This herb works by increasing the levels of a neurotransmitter called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, which helps to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. Maypop has been used traditionally for its calming effects and is now being studied for its potential role in treating anxiety disorders. It provides a more natural alternative to traditional anxiety medications, which can have unwanted side effects.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Maypop contains active constituents such as passionflower alkaloids and flavonoids that help reduce anxiety by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing nervousness.

Sedative: The sedative properties of maypop, attributed to its harman alkaloids, help alleviate anxiety by calming the nervous system, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep for those experiencing anxiety-related insomnia.

Antidepressant: Maypop contains active constituents like chrysin and benzoflavone, which have been shown to have antidepressant effects, potentially helping to improve mood and reduce anxiety in individuals with comorbid depression.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of maypop, due to its alkaloids and flavonoids, help relax muscles and alleviate tension, which can contribute to anxiety relief in individuals experiencing muscle tension or spasms.

Adaptogenic: Maypop has adaptogenic properties that support the body's response to stress, allowing it to better cope with anxiety-inducing situations, thanks to its diverse range of active constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the maypop capsule plant are often used in medicinal tea for anxiety due to their natural sedative properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the maypop capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for anxiety because they contain compounds that help promote relaxation and reduce tension.
Flowers: The flowers of the maypop capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for anxiety due to their calming effects and ability to help alleviate stress.
Seeds: The seeds of the maypop capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for anxiety because they contain compounds that help regulate mood and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2,000-3,000 dried maypop (Passiflora incarnata) leaves and stems, equating to approximately 2-3 pounds.
Thoroughly clean and sanitize a spacious drying area and grinder, ensuring no cross-contamination occurs.
Dry the maypop leaves and stems at 100-110°F for 4-6 hours, or until they become crisp and crumble easily.
Grind the dried maypop leaves and stems into a fine powder using the sanitized grinder.
Fill size "00" vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the maypop powder, creating a standard dosage for anxiety relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsule helps with anxiety because it contains valerenic acid and other compounds that may have a calming effect on the brain. These compounds may increase the amount of a neurotransmitter called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, which has a sedative effect. This can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. Additionally, valerian may have an effect on other neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which can also play a role in anxiety. Overall, valerian's ability to increase GABA and potentially affect other neurotransmitters make it a helpful option for those looking for a natural way to manage anxiety.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Valerian capsules can help reduce anxiety by acting on GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing nervousness (active constituents

Sedative: The sedative properties of valerian help alleviate anxiety symptoms by inducing calmness and facilitating sleep, making it easier for individuals to manage anxiety-related insomnia (active constituents

Antidepressant: Valerian has mild antidepressant effects that can help alleviate anxiety symptoms by regulating mood and emotional responses, reducing the likelihood of anxiety-induced depressive episodes (active constituents
Muscle relaxant: Valerian's muscle relaxant properties can help reduce anxiety-induced muscle tension, promoting physical relaxation and easing discomfort associated with anxiety (active constituents

Neuroprotective: Valerian has neuroprotective properties that can help protect the brain from anxiety-induced damage, promoting overall brain health and resilience against anxiety-related stress (active constituents
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the valerian plant contain high levels of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making it effective for reducing anxiety.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes are rich in essential oils and alkaloids, contributing to the plant's sedative and anxiolytic properties, making them suitable for medicinal tea.
Flowers: Valerian flowers have a milder sedative effect compared to the roots and rhizomes, and can be used in combination with them to create a more balanced and less potent anxiety-relieving tea.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried valerian root, size 00 vegetarian capsules, and a capsule filling machine.
Grind 2 tablespoons of valerian root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure 500mg of valerian root powder per capsule using a digital scale.
Fill the capsules with the powder using the filling machine and let them dry for 24 hours.
Store the completed capsules in a tightly sealed container in a cool, dry place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap capsule helps with anxiety because it contains active compounds that can help promote relaxation and reduce anxiety symptoms. These compounds include flavonoids, volatile oils, and bitter iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative effects. Skullcap may help to regulate mood by interacting with neurotransmitters such as dopamine and serotonin, which play a role in regulating mood and emotional responses. Additionally, skullcap may help to reduce symptoms of anxiety by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality. Overall, skullcap capsule can be a natural and effective way to help manage anxiety symptoms.
remedy_properties: Nervine: Skullcap has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils such as scutellarin and baicalin.

Anxiolytic: Skullcap has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm, making it useful for managing symptoms of anxiety disorders. This is attributed to the presence of antioxidant compounds like wogonin and oroxylin A.

Antidepressant: Skullcap has been found to have antidepressant effects, which can help alleviate anxiety and improve mood. The active constituents responsible for this property include phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Sedative: Skullcap has sedative properties, which can help reduce anxiety and promote sleep. This is due to the presence of volatile oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids such as tannins and catalpol.

Adaptogenic: Skullcap has adaptogenic properties, which help the body cope with stress and reduce anxiety. This is attributed to the presence of various active constituents, including flavonoids, saponins, and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the skullcap capsule plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for anxiety due to their calming and relaxing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the skullcap capsule plant are used in tea for anxiety because they contain compounds that may help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Seeds: The seeds of the skullcap capsule plant are sometimes used in tea for anxiety because they may have anti-anxiety effects and may help improve mood.
Stems: The stems of the skullcap capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for anxiety because they contain compounds that may help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried skullcap herb and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the skullcap powder with an equal amount of filler material, such as powdered rice or oat flour, to make it easier to handle and form into capsules.
Load the skullcap-filler mixture into size "00" gelatin capsules using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Take 1-2 capsules up to three times a day as needed, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus capsule helps with anxiety because it contains natural compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system. This plant, also known as hops, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The active ingredients in hops, such as bitter acids and essential oils, have been shown to have anxiolytic effects, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Additionally, humulus lupulus may increase the levels of certain neurotransmitters in the brain, such as gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), which has a calming effect on the nervous system. Overall, humulus lupulus capsule can be a natural and effective way to manage anxiety and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Humulus lupulus (hops) capsules can help reduce anxiety due to the presence of active constituents such as bitter acids and essential oils, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system.

Sedative: Hops capsules possess sedative properties, which can help alleviate anxiety by promoting relaxation and sleep, thanks to the presence of active compounds like dimethylvinyl carbinol and 2-methyl-3-buten-2-ol.
Nervine: Humulus lupulus has nervine properties that support the nervous system, helping to soothe nerves and reduce anxiety, partly due to the action of flavonoids and volatile oils found in hops.

Antidepressant: Hops capsules may help alleviate anxiety by acting as a mild antidepressant, potentially due to the presence of xanthohumol and other prenylated flavonoids that can influence neurotransmitter levels in the brain.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of hops, attributed to compounds like humulones and lupulones, can contribute to reducing anxiety by alleviating inflammation in the body, which is often associated with stress and anxiety disorders.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the humulus lupulus plant, also known as hops, contain relaxing properties that can help alleviate anxiety.
Leaves: The leaves of the hop plant have calming effects and are often used in teas to help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Stems: The stems of the hop plant contain essential oils that have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them useful for reducing anxiety.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the hop plant contain active compounds that have a sedative effect, making them useful for reducing anxiety and promoting sleep.
Buds: The buds of the hop plant are rich in essential oils that have a calming effect on the mind and can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 grams of dried Humulus lupulus (hops) flowers and 500mg of passionflower herb.
Grind the hops flowers and passionflower herb separately into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the hops and passionflower powders in a small bowl, mixing thoroughly.
Fill size 00 empty vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the finished hops and passionflower capsules in an airtight container, away from direct sunlight, for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsule helps with anxiety because it contains a potent extract derived from the Lavandula angustifolia plant, which has been used traditionally for its calming and relaxing properties. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, are believed to interact with the brain's neurotransmitters, potentially increasing GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) activity, which helps regulate excitability in the nervous system. This, in turn, may promote a sense of tranquility, reduce nervous tension, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety, making English lavender capsules a popular natural alternative for managing anxiety.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: English lavender has been shown to reduce anxiety by increasing GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) activity in the brain, which helps regulate nerve impulses, promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness. This is primarily due to the presence of linalool and linalyl acetate in lavender essential oil.

Sedative: Lavender has sedative properties that can help alleviate anxiety by promoting better sleep quality and reducing insomnia. The main constituents responsible for this effect are linalool and lavandulin.

Nervous system relaxant: English lavender has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety by decreasing heart rate and blood pressure. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, linalyl acetate, and terpinen-4-ol.

Antidepressant: Lavender has been found to have antidepressant properties, which can help alleviate anxiety by improving mood and reducing depressive symptoms. This is attributed to the presence of linalool, linalyl acetate, and 1,8-cineole.

Anti-inflammatory: English lavender has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce anxiety by alleviating physical symptoms associated with stress, such as muscle tension and headaches. The main active constituents responsible for this property include caryophyllene oxide, linalool, and linalyl acetate.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in essential oils, the flowers of English lavender are commonly used in herbal teas for anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain similar constituents as the flowers, and are often used in tea blends for anxiety to provide a well-rounded, calming effect.
Buds: High in volatile oils, lavender buds contribute to the tea's aroma and therapeutic qualities, aiding in relaxation and stress reduction.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried English lavender flowers and 0.5-1 teaspoon of lemon balm leaves, both finely ground.
Combine the ground herbs with 0.5-1 teaspoon of passionflower and 0.5-1 teaspoon of chamomile flowers in a small bowl.
Mix the herbal blend well, then measure out 500-650mg per gelatin capsule, filling approximately 30-40 capsules.
Use a capsule machine or manually fill and compress the gelatin capsules, storing in a cool, dry place.
Take 1-2 capsules up to three times daily, as needed for anxiety relief, with food or water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsule helps with anxiety because it contains rosmarinic acid and eugenol, which have been found to have calming effects on the brain and can help reduce anxiety symptoms. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, a neurotransmitter that helps regulate anxiety. Furthermore, it may also help alleviate symptoms of anxiety by promoting relaxation and improving mood. Studies have suggested that lemon balm can help reduce symptoms of stress and anxiety, making it a natural alternative for those looking to manage anxiety.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Lemon balm contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anxiolytic effects by reducing GABA uptake, increasing GABA levels in the brain, and binding to GABA receptors, thus promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Sedative: Lemon balm contains eugenol and tannins, which have sedative properties, making it helpful in reducing anxiety by promoting sleep and alleviating insomnia.
Antidepressant: Lemon balm contains terpene alcohols, which have antidepressant effects, making it helpful in reducing anxiety by uplifting mood and alleviating depressive symptoms.

Antioxidant: Lemon balm contains rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have antioxidant properties, making it helpful in reducing anxiety by protecting the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation.

Neuroprotective: Lemon balm contains eugenol and rosmarinic acid, which have neuroprotective effects, making it helpful in reducing anxiety by protecting the brain from damage and promoting cognitive health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used to make medicinal tea for anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have a relaxing effect on the nervous system, making them a popular choice for anxiety relief.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm can still contribute to the tea's calming effect and add to its distinctive lemon-mint flavor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves, or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves, and rinse them thoroughly.
Grind the lemon balm leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 500 mg of the lemon balm powder and mix it with an appropriate binder (e.g., microcrystalline cellulose) in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill size 00 gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the lemon balm-binder mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the finished lemon balm capsules in a cool, dry place, and take 1-2 capsules up to three times daily as needed for anxiety relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsule helps with anxiety because it contains active compounds like rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce anxiety symptoms. These compounds may help regulate mood and promote relaxation by interacting with neurotransmitters in the brain, such as GABA and serotonin. Additionally, sage has been used traditionally for its calming properties and may help alleviate stress and promote mental clarity. Overall, sage capsules can be a natural and effective way to help manage anxiety symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-anxiety effects: Sage capsules contain compounds such as rosmarinic acid and various terpene compounds that have been shown to help reduce anxiety levels by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain.
Nervous system support: Sage contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help protect the nervous system from damage, which can contribute to anxiety symptoms.
Cognitive enhancement: Sage has been shown to improve memory and attention, which can help reduce anxiety related to cognitive decline or memory loss.
Hormonal balance: Sage can help regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to anxiety, particularly in women experiencing menopause-related anxiety.
Mood support: Sage contains compounds that can help regulate mood and alleviate symptoms of depression, which often co-occur with anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the sage capsule plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the sage capsule plant are used in tea for their mild sedative effects, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Seeds: The seeds of the sage capsule plant are used in tea for their high antioxidant content, which can help reduce stress and anxiety by protecting cells from damage.
Bark: The bark of the sage capsule plant is used in tea for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce anxiety by reducing inflammation in the body.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried sage leaves and 1/2 cup of empty gelatin capsules.
Grind the sage leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill each gelatin capsule with the sage powder using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed sage capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place for up to six months.
Take one to two capsules daily, as needed, with a full glass of water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: California poppy capsule helps with anxiety because it contains bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids, that interact with the body's natural chemical messengers, including neurotransmitters, to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety symptoms. The herb has been traditionally used for its calming and soothing effects on the nervous system, helping to alleviate feelings of stress, tension, and unease. By promoting a sense of calm and well-being, California poppy capsules can help individuals manage anxiety and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic activity: California poppy contains alkaloids such as protopine and allocryptopine, which have been shown to have anxiety-reducing effects, making it helpful in managing anxiety symptoms.
Sedative properties: The isoquinoline alkaloids found in California poppy have sedative effects, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety by calming the nervous system.
Neuroprotective effects: California poppy has neuroprotective properties, which may help protect the brain from damage caused by chronic stress and anxiety, supporting overall brain health.
Pain relief: Containing opium-like alkaloids, California poppy can help alleviate physical pain, a common symptom associated with anxiety disorders, thus improving overall well-being.

Improved sleep quality: The sedative and anxiolytic properties of California poppy can improve sleep quality, as it can help users fall asleep faster and experience deeper, more restorative sleep, reducing anxiety-related insomnia.
remedy_parts: Flowers: California poppy flowers are commonly used in medicinal tea for anxiety due to their mild sedative properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the California poppy plant are used in tea to help promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Seeds: California poppy seeds can be used in tea to help induce sleep and alleviate anxiety.
Stems: The stems of the California poppy plant can be used in tea to help treat nervousness and restlessness.
Roots: Although not as commonly used as the other parts, the roots of the California poppy plant can be used in tea to help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 0.5-1 teaspoon of dried California poppy petals and mix with 1 cup of a binding agent, such as powdered cellulose or rice flour.
Combine the mixture in a bowl and add a small amount of water, just enough to create a damp, moldable consistency.
Form the mixture into small, equal-sized capsules using a capsule maker or by hand.
Allow the capsules to dry for 24-48 hours in a cool, dark place.
Store the dried capsules in an airtight container and take 1-2 capsules up to three times daily as needed for anxiety relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jujube capsule helps with anxiety because it contains compounds that may have a calming effect on the nervous system. Jujube, also known as Ziziphus jujuba, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat anxiety and promote sleep. The fruit contains saponins, flavonoids, and other compounds that have been shown to have anxiolytic effects. Studies have suggested that jujube may help to regulate the levels of certain neurotransmitters in the brain, such as serotonin and dopamine, which can have a positive impact on mood and anxiety levels. Additionally, jujube has been shown to have a sedative effect, which may help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic effect: Jujube capsules have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. This is partly due to the presence of saponins, which have been shown to have a sedative effect.

Adaptogenic properties: Jujube helps the body adapt to stress, which can help alleviate anxiety. This is attributed to the active constituent jujuboside A, which has been found to have stress-relieving effects.
Sedative effect: Jujube can help improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted in cases of anxiety. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, which have sedative properties.
Neuroprotective effects: Jujube can protect the brain from damage, which can contribute to anxiety. This is thanks to the antioxidant properties of jujube, which help to reduce oxidative stress.

Regulation of neurotransmitters: Jujube can help regulate the levels of neurotransmitters in the brain, including serotonin and dopamine, which are often imbalanced in cases of anxiety. This is due to the presence of alkaloids, which can influence neurotransmitter activity.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Jujube seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for anxiety due to their sedative and calming properties.
Flowers: Jujube flowers are used in tea to help alleviate anxiety by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
Leaves: Jujube leaves are used in medicinal tea to treat anxiety by reducing stress and regulating mood.
Fruits: Jujube fruits are used in tea to help manage anxiety by nourishing the nervous system and promoting overall well-being.
Bark: Jujube bark is used in tea for anxiety relief by soothing the mind and regulating the body's response to stress.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of jujube fruit, 10 grams of chamomile flowers, and 10 grams of passionflower herb, all dried and finely ground.
Combine the ground jujube, chamomile, and passionflower in a bowl, mixing thoroughly.
Using a capsule machine, fill size "00" vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture, creating approximately 40-50 capsules.
Store the completed herbal jujube capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.
Take one to two capsules, as needed, up to three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, to help manage anxiety symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet flag capsule helps with anxiety because it contains active compounds that have anxiolytic and sedative properties. These compounds work by regulating the neurotransmitters in the brain, particularly gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), which is responsible for promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. Additionally, sweet flag has been shown to have a calming effect on the central nervous system, helping to alleviate symptoms of anxiety such as restlessness, irritability, and insomnia. Furthermore, sweet flag has been used traditionally in Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine to treat various mental health disorders, including anxiety and depression.
remedy_properties: Anti-anxiety properties: Sweet flag capsules contain active constituents such as essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate anxiety symptoms.

Nervine tonic properties: Sweet flag capsules contain alkaloids that act as nervine tonics, helping to soothe and strengthen the nervous system, which is often compromised in individuals experiencing anxiety.

Antidepressant properties: The active constituents in sweet flag capsules, including essential oils and alkaloids, have been shown to have antidepressant effects, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms and improve mood.

Cognitive-enhancing properties: Sweet flag capsules contain compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function, including memory and attention, which can be impaired in individuals experiencing anxiety.
Adaptogenic properties: Sweet flag capsules contain adaptogenic compounds that help the body adapt to stress, which can help reduce anxiety symptoms and improve overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the sweet flag plant contain many therapeutic compounds, making them a popular choice for medicinal tea to help alleviate anxiety symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the sweet flag plant are often used in tea due to their calming properties, which may help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Flowers: The flowers of the sweet flag plant contain volatile oils and other beneficial compounds, making them useful in tea for managing anxiety and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried sweet flag (Acorus calamus) root and grind it into a powder, which will take about 5 minutes.
Mix the ground sweet flag root with 1 teaspoon of empty vegetarian capsule bodies, blending thoroughly.
Using a capsule filling machine or by hand, fill the vegetarian capsule bodies with the sweet flag root powder.
Seal the filled capsules and store in a cool, dry place, ensuring they are out of direct sunlight.
Take 1-2 capsules daily as needed, preferably with a meal.
